sections.summary: Document title: Standard for Whole Maize (Corn) Meal — heading only, not substantive regulatory text.
Standard identifier CXS 154-1985 — document code heading, not substantive.

sections.section: Adopted in 1985. Revised in 1995. Amended in 2019, 2023.
sections.summary: Adoption history: Adopted 1985, revised 1995, amended 2019 and 2023 — informational, not substantive requirements.
Notice that amendments were made to the Annex following decisions at the Forty‑sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023.

Scope: applies to whole maize (corn) meal for direct human consumption prepared from Zea mays L. kernels and explicitly excludes degermed or enriched maize meals, maize flours and various grits and ready‑to‑eat maize products, as well as maize for brewing, starch/industrial uses or animal feed.

Description: defines whole maize (corn) meal as food prepared from fully mature, sound, ungerminated whole maize kernels by grinding the entire grain to a suitable fineness, allowing coarse particles to be separated, reground and recombined.

Sets out essential composition and quality factors for whole maize meal, including general safety and freedom from defects and a specific moisture limit of 15.0% m/m with allowance that lower moisture limits may be required for certain destinations due to climate, transport and storage duration.

States general quality requirements for whole maize meal: it must be safe and suitable for human consumption, free from abnormal flavours/odours and living insects, and free from filth (animal-origin impurities, including dead insects) in amounts that may hazard human health.

sections.section: 3.1.1 Whole maize meal shall be safe and suitable for human consumption.
sections.summary: Requires that whole maize meal be safe and suitable for human consumption.

sections.section: 3.1.2 Whole maize meal shall be free from abnormal flavours, odours, and living insects.
sections.summary: Prohibits whole maize meal from having abnormal flavours or odours and from containing living insects.

sections.section: 3.1.3 Whole maize meal shall be free from filth  (impurities of animal origin,  including dead  insects) in amounts which may represent a hazard to human health.
sections.summary: Requires whole maize meal to be free from filth (impurities of animal origin, including dead insects) in amounts that may represent a hazard to human health.

Specifies a maximum moisture content of 15.0% m/m for whole maize meal and notes that lower moisture limits may be required for particular destinations depending on climate and transport/storage duration.
Sets maximum moisture content for whole maize meal at 15.0% m/m and advises that lower limits may be needed for certain destinations based on climate and transport/storage duration.

Requires whole maize meal to be free from heavy metals at hazardous levels and to comply with Codex maximum residue limits for pesticide residues and with Codex mycotoxin limits applicable to this commodity.
Requires whole maize meal to be free from heavy metals in amounts that may represent a hazard to human health.
Requires whole maize meal to comply with Codex Alimentarius Commission maximum residue limits for pesticide residues applicable to this commodity.
Requires whole maize meal to comply with Codex Alimentarius Commission maximum mycotoxin limits established for this commodity.

Recommends preparing and handling whole maize meal in accordance with the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene and relevant codes of practice and, under good manufacturing practice, requires the product to be free from objectionable matter and, when tested, from microorganisms, parasites and substances originating from microorganisms in amounts that may pose a health hazard.

sections.section: 5.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance  with  the  appropriate  sections  of  the General  Principles  of  Food  Hygiene (CXC  1-1969) 1   and other  codes  of  practice  recommended  by  the  Codex  Alimentarius  Commission  which  are  relevant  to  this product.
sections.summary: Recommends that whole maize meal be prepared and handled following the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and other relevant Codex codes of practice.

sections.section: 5.2 To the extent possible in good manufacturing practice, the product shall be free from objectionable matter.
sections.summary: Requires, to the extent possible under good manufacturing practice, that whole maize meal be free from objectionable matter.

sections.section: 5.3 When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product:
sections.summary: When tested by appropriate sampling and examination methods, whole maize meal must be free from microorganisms and parasites in amounts that may represent a hazard and must not contain substances originating from microorganisms in hazardous amounts.

Requires packaging for whole maize meal to safeguard hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities, to be made of safe materials that do not impart toxic substances or undesirable odours/flavours, and specifies that sacks, when used, must be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.

sections.section: 6.1 Whole  maize  (corn)  meal  shall  be  packaged  in  containers  which  will  safeguard  the  hygienic,  nutritional, technological, and organoleptic qualities of the product.
sections.summary: Requires whole maize meal to be packaged in containers that safeguard its hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities.

sections.section: 6.2 The containers, including packaging material, shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their  intended  use.  They  should  not  impart  any  toxic  substance  or  undesirable  odour  or  flavour  to  the product.
sections.summary: Requires packaging materials and containers for whole maize meal to be made of substances safe and suitable for their intended use and not to impart toxic substances or undesirable odours or flavours to the product.

Advises users to specify the appropriate factor limits and methods when more than one is given and sets analytical limits: ash max 3.0% (dry weight), protein (N×6.25) min 8% (dry weight), crude fat min 3.1% (dry weight), and granularity requirements (≥95% through 1.70 mm; ≥45% through 0.71 mm; ≤35% through 0.212 mm).
